data.title: India's telecom regulator recommends allotting satellite spectrum for 5 years
data.description: India's telecom regulator has recommended the allotment of satellite spectrum for commercial communication services for a period of five years, it said on Friday, adding that the government may extend this for a further period of two years.

data.title: China Rare Earth Completes HK$15.5 Million Share Sale
data.description:
China Rare Earth completed the sale of 48,750,000 shares at HK$0.32 apiece to Double Bay Capital as of Thursday, May 8, according to a same-day filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The sale, first announced in April, saw net proceeds of HK$15.5 million, according to an April 16 filing.

The shares are equivalent to 2% of the share capital of China Rare Earth.

data.title: Croatia Producer Prices Down 0.5% in April
data.description:
Producer prices in Croatia dropped by 0.5% year-on-year in April 2025, after a flat reading in the previous month.

The decline was mainly due to a decrease in costs for energy (-5.1% vs -3.4% in March) and a slowdown in prices for capital goods (2% vs 2.2%) and durable consumer goods (0.7% vs 0.8%).

Meanwhile, producer inflation remained unchanged for intermediate goods (at 1.5%) and non-durable consumer goods (at 2.1%).

Excluding energy, producer prices stood at 1.8% in April, unchanged from the prior month.

On a monthly basis, producer prices went down by 1.5%, following a 0.4% fall in the preceding period.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Rises. Why It's Ending a Good Week With More Gains. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

Nvidia stock rose early Friday as the artificial intelligence chip maker looked set to end a strong week with more gains, keeping its recent momentum going.

Shares, which are up 2.5% this week through Thursday's close, rose 0.4% to $117.88 in premarket trading. Futures tracking the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were up 0.2%.

Nvidia stock has jumped 7.8% so far in May amid renewed optimism over trade and tariffs. Earlier this week the Trump administration said it would scrap regulations limiting global exports of advanced chips, drawn up by the Biden administration.

Nvidia is close to releasing a downgraded version of its H20 chip for China, Reuters reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The H20 chip is specifically designed for the Chinese market to comply with U.S. export restrictions.

It comes after Nvidia last month said future sales of its H20 chips to China would require a license from the U.S. Department of Commerce, effectively blocking sales of those chips as licenses are unlikely to be forthcoming.

Nvidia didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

Changhong Huayi Compressor to Buy Back Up to 300 Million Yuan of Shares
data.description:
Changhong Huayi Compressor is planning to buy back between 150 million yuan and 300 million yuan of its shares via centralized bidding.

The shares will be repurchased at up to 9.8 yuan per share within 12 months from board approvals, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the manufacturer of components for refrigerator and freezer compressors closed less than 2% higher Friday.

data.title: DDM Unit Plans Capital Raise to Support European NPL Acquisitions
data.description:
DDM Holding (DDM.ST) unit DDM Debt intends to issue new shares to raise capital for the planned revival of its non-performing loan, or NPL, purchasing activities in the European markets.

The Swedish investor said Friday the funds will be used to speed up its acquisition of secured and unsecured NPL portfolios across Centraland Eastern Europe, as well as grow its assets and rebuild its Estimated Remaining Collections base, among others.

data.title: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) contract prices slip in Europe
data.description: This week, PVC contract prices declined, while spot prices remained constant in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "In May, the European polyvinyl chloride market saw a reduction in contract prices due to a decrease in the May contract price set by the European ethylene industry, alongside persistently low demand for PVC. Both producers and consumers anticipate that market conditions in May will mirror those of April, characterized by gradual yet stable demand and slight supply constraints. Buyers have observed a lack of increase in downstream demand, attributed to subdued construction activity and consumer spending. Following the price settlement, negotiations commenced in the PVC contract market between producers and consumers. Producers emphasized the necessity of improving margins, describing current margins as unsustainable. Conversely, consumers advocated for more significant price reductions in light of the declining ethylene market contract price for May and the weak PVC demand from downstream sectors. Looking ahead, industry sources predict a rise in demand in May compared to April, although the Easter holidays are believed to have dampened market activity.Supply in Europe remains somewhat limited, with outages reported in both France and Poland. A facility in Pernis, Netherlands, is expected to restart operations, with PVC deliveries anticipated for May. A site in France is projected to resume production by the end of May, while a facility in Poland is currently undergoing maintenance following a technical issue during its restart.This week, offers in the spot market have decreased, particularly for Chinese materials, which are now more affordable as compared to other Asian materials from Korea and Taiwan. As regards imports, a varied situation has emerged; some sources reported diminished interest due to the decline in the European ethylene MCP for May. Conversely, other market participants noted that the recent appreciation of the euro against the US dollar, coupled with weak domestic demand in Asia, has created more appealing opportunities. Increased interest in imported grades has been observed this week from buyers who were previously hesitant about importing volumes.In Spain, market conditions are stabilizing following the power outage; however, sources indicate that some supply disruptions persist. In Turkey, weak demand continues amid a challenging political and economic environment. European producers have maintained lower pricing since the beginning of the year to remain competitive against affordably priced materials from the US and Asia.The downward trend in crude oil prices is contributing to lower energy costs and affecting the overall petrochemicals market, which is expected to lower production costs in May. This situation may also encourage a more cautious approach among petrochemical buyers, who anticipate that price reductions will eventually be reflected in downstream markets. Meanwhile, demand for Emulsion PVC (e-PVC) in northwest Europe remains weak, particularly as the automotive industry, a key end-use market for e-PVC, has faced increasing pressure in recent months.In the contract markets, PVC Suspension grade prices were assessed at the Euro 970-975/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE France levels, both falling by Euro (-10/mt) from last week. PVC Suspension grade prices were assessed at the Euro 980-985/mt FD NWE Italy levels, a week on week drop of Euro (-10/mt). Meanwhile, PVC prices were assessed at the GBP 825-830/mt FD NWE UK levels, down GBP (-5/mt) from the previous week.In the spot markets, FD North West Europe PVC prices were assessed at the Euro 895-905/mt levels, steady from the previous week. Meanwhile, feedstock EDC prices were assessed at the USD 175-185/mt FOB North West Europe levels while VCM prices were assessed at the USD 515-525/mt FOB North West Europe levels, both unchanged week on week.Ethylene spot prices on Thursday were assessed at the Euro 735-745/mt FD North West Europe levels, a decrease of Euro (-20/mt) from last week.European ethylene contract price for May 2025 settled at the Euro 1135/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a plunge of Euro 70/MT from its April 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: DBRS Lifts India Rating To BBB, Changes Outlook to Stable
data.description:
DBRS Morningstar upgraded its long-term foreign issuer ratings on India to BBB from BBB (low) and changed its outlook to stable from positive on May 8,2025, citing the cumulative benefits of structural reforms that are driving fiscal consolidation and sustaining strong growth.

The agency said that the upgrade also reflects a more resilient banking sector, with non-performing loans at a 13-year low of 2.5%.

Reforms, infrastructure investment, and rapid digitalization have fueled a post-pandemic recovery, with GDP averaging 8.2% from FY22 to FY25.

Fiscal transparency and spending quality have improved, supporting the upgrade alongside gains in fiscal and monetary policy management.

data.title: Corvus Pharma Reports 'Favorable' Interim Phase 1 Results in Atopic Dermatitis Trial; Shares Surge Premarket
data.description:
Corvus Pharmaceuticals said late Thursday that new interim data from a phase 1 clinical trial of soquelitinib to treat moderate to severe atopic dermatitis showed a "favorable" safety and efficacy profile.

The company found "earlier and deeper responses" in group 3, which received 200 milligrams twice a day, compared with groups 1 and 2, which received 100 mg twice daily and 200 mg once daily.

Corvus said that all three groups showed "significant responses" in the soquelitinib treatment groups, compared with placebo for clinically significant endpoints.

Atopic dermatitis is a skin condition characterized by dry, itchy, and inflamed skin.

Shares of the company were up 37% in recent premarket activity Friday.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Global Markets Mixed as Investors Look Toward U.S., China Talks

Stock markets globally were mixed with U.S. futures pretty much flat following Thursday's bounce after the U.S.-U.K. trade deal.

China's Exports to U.S. Plunge, in Sign of Bite From Trump Tariffs

The drop in U.S.-bound shipments was offset by a surge in Chinese goods to Southeast Asia, Africa, Europe and Latin America.

BOJ Rate Hike Unlikely Until Trade Uncertainty Clears

The Bank of Japan will likely hold fire for the next few months due to growing concerns about the impact of U.S. tariffs, a former member of the BOJ's policy board says.

Trump's Bespoke Trade Deal With U.K. Sets Little Precedent for Other Nations

Many other agreements weren't seen as likely to come together quickly.

With U.K. Deal, U.S. Signals That 10% Tariff on World Is New Baseline

Both countries are hailing this as a good outcome, showing how much the landscape has shifted.

Bitcoin Price, XRP Soar. Here's the Risk to the Crypto Rally.

While Bitcoin and other cryptos have seen impressive double digit gains this week there are risks to the rally.

RBA to Walk Rate-Cut Path With Dangers on Both Sides

The Reserve Bank of Australia is set to cut rates and keep on cutting until the official cash rate is at a far less restrictive level.

New York Fed Survey Finds Consumers Ambivalent on Inflation Outlook

Consumers sent mixed signals about their inflation expectations in the New York Fed's April survey. But the poll showed no serious rise in long-term inflation expectations.

Canada's Financial Stability Faces Stiff Test From U.S. Tariffs, Bank of Canada Says

Annual central bank report says there is a risk permanent tariffs could cause high unemployment and business insolvencies.

EU Targets American Aircraft, Car Parts for Possible Tariffs if Talks Fail

The bloc said it could target more than $100 billion worth of American products with tariffs if negotiations with the U.S. break down.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Panasonic Holdings to Cut 10,000 Jobs Globally

The Japanese electronics maker said it would re-evaluate the numbers of organizations and personnel needed.

Commerzbank Posts Higher Earnings After Commission Income Lifts Revenue

Earnings and revenue for the first quarter exceeded analyst expectations, after higher commission income offset a decline in interest income.

IAG to Buy 53 Planes Despite Market Uncertainty

British Airways owner International Consolidated Airlines said it ordered the planes to bolster its long-haul fleet after earnings rose.

Coinbase Reports Drop in Profit

Earlier in the day Coinbase announced a $2.9 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire Deribit, a bitcoin options exchange.

McKesson to Spin Off Medical-Surgical Company

The pharmaceutical distributor said the new company would be a differentiated medical surgical supply and solutions business.

Lyft Swings to Profit Amid Increase in Ridership

San Francisco-based company said rides grew 16% year over year, to 218.4 million, ahead of analyst views for 215.1 million.

CoStar Agrees to Buy Australian Advertiser Domain for $1.9 Billion

U.S. real-estate analytics provider CoStar agreed to buy property advertiser Domain in a sweetened deal.

News Corp Revenue Boosted by Gains at Dow Jones, HarperCollins

Digital and print Wall Street Journal subscriptions totaled 4.34 million for the March quarter, the company says.

Peloton Slashes Marketing Again in Hard Play for Profitability

Double-digit percentage cuts continue for the fourth consecutive quarter as the fitness company's marketing chief leaves.

TKO Group Raises Full-Year Outlook on Recent Acquisitions, Swings to Profit

The new forecasts came as TKO Group swung to a profit and posted higher-than-expected revenue in the recent quarter.
